description: Fix the IAM policy ARN for Bedrock InvokeModel permission and increase Lambda timeout. The model ID 'eu.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0' uses the 'eu.' prefix indicating it is a cross-region inference profile, NOT a foundation model. The ARN must use 'inference-profile' resource type with account field populated (use '*'). Also increase Lambda timeout from 30s to 60s to accommodate URL fetch (8s timeout) + Bedrock inference time.
steps: In apps/infra/src/stacks/recipe-manager-stack.ts, change the Bedrock IAM policy resource from 'arn:aws:bedrock:*::foundation-model/eu.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0' to 'arn:aws:bedrock:*:*:inference-profile/eu.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0'. The key differences: resource type changes from 'foundation-model' to 'inference-profile', and the account field changes from empty (::) to wildcard (*:*:).
In the same file, increase the Lambda function timeout from Duration.seconds(30) to Duration.seconds(60) to give the import flow enough headroom for URL fetch plus Bedrock inference.
acceptance_criteria: The IAM policy resource ARN uses 'inference-profile' resource type instead of 'foundation-model'
The IAM policy resource ARN has '*' in the account field (arn:aws:bedrock:*:*:inference-profile/...)
The Lambda timeout is set to 60 seconds
The infra project builds successfully with `pnpm nx build infra`
verification: Run `pnpm nx build infra` to confirm the TypeScript compiles without errors
Run `pnpm nx lint infra` to confirm no linting issues
findings: Both changes applied cleanly. Build and lint pass without issues. No tests exist in the project currently.
